title: Machine Learning Systems Engineer
text:
I am a dual Master’s student at Purdue University (ECE & IE) with a deep interest in Machine Learning Systems, High-Performance Computing, and AI Infrastructure. I am passionate about the “full-stack” nature of modern AI—exploring the critical bridge where model architecture meets compiler design and hardware acceleration. My work is grounded in a strong architectural understanding of the deep learning landscape, from foundational CNNs and LSTMs to state-of-the-art Transformers, VLMs, and Diffusion models.
I am particularly excited by the “systems journey” of these workloads. My projects revolve around analyzing how high-level abstractions in frameworks like PyTorch are captured as computational graphs, lowered through various Intermediate Representations (IRs), and ultimately compiled down to hardware-specific instruction sets (such as PTX, SASS, or machine code). I enjoy working on challenges related to inference and training optimization, including KV-cache management, quantization, operator fusion, and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) execution. Through my work with CUDA C++ and Triton, I actively build custom kernels that leverage tiling strategies and memory hierarchies, while also exploring scalable paradigms for distributed and disaggregated inference across diverse hardware platforms.
I am seeking opportunities in machine learning systems, infrastructure, and performance-oriented engineering roles, where I can contribute to building reliable, efficient, and scalable AI systems. This website highlights selected projects and technical writing that reflect how I approach system design, optimization, and problem-solving.

title: Resume
text:
Technical Focus

 GPU Performance CUDA C++, Triton, memory hierarchy, tiling, profiling, and kernel optimization 
 Inference Systems KV cache, continuous batching, speculative decoding, quantization, and serving efficiency 
 Compilers & Frameworks Graph capture, IR lowering, operator fusion, autograd, and PyTorch-style framework internals 
 Distributed AI Collectives, tensor/pipeline/expert/context parallelism, process groups, and topology-aware serving

title: DL Framework
text:
Building a compact PyTorch-style deep learning framework to understand tensors, autograd, modules, dispatch, and native performance paths.


  Python Tensor Library Tensor object, broadcasting, indexing, reductions, and NumPy-backed operations 
  Autograd Engine Dynamic computation graph, reverse-mode differentiation, and gradient checks 
  Neural Network Modules Parameter management, layers, losses, optimizers, and training loops 
  C++ Backend Native tensor kernels, Python bindings, memory layout, and extension interfaces

title: Projects
text: Selected project areas across GPU performance, deep learning runtimes, compiler stacks, model architectures, and end-to-end ML systems work.

title: Projects
text:
Featured Systems Work

 Kernel Performance GPU Kernel Benchmarks  Operator-level CUDA, Triton, and CuTe work focused on memory bandwidth, tiling, occupancy, and measurable speedups.  LayerNorm · GEMM · Vector Ops 
 Framework Internals Mini PyTorch-Style Framework  A compact framework path from tensors and reverse-mode autograd to modules, native kernels, and Python/C++ boundaries.  Tensors · Autograd · C++ Backend 
 Scale-Out Inference Distributed Inference Parallelism  Communication primitives and model-parallel execution for multi-GPU serving, including collectives, TP, PP, EP, CP, and placement.  Collectives · TP/PP/EP · Multi-node

title: Projects
text:
Project Areas

 Performance GPU Kernels  CUDA C++, Triton, and CuTe DSL kernels for deep learning operators and memory-bound workloads.   CUDA C++ Triton CuTe DSL  
 Serving Runtime  Runtime systems work around vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT, and TensorRT-LLM.   vLLM SGLang TensorRT  
 Frameworks DL Framework  Building a small PyTorch-style framework in Python and C++ with tensors, autograd, modules, and kernels.   Python C++ Autograd  
 Compilation DL Compilers  Graph capture, IR lowering, fusion, scheduling, and compiler pipelines for tensor programs.   MLIR XLA torch.compile  
 Measurement Profiling  Nsight Systems, Nsight Compute, PyTorch Profiler, memory traces, and reproducible bottleneck analysis.   Nsight PyTorch Perfetto  
 Efficiency Inference Optimization  Quantization, KV cache reuse, prefix caching, batching, speculative decoding, and serving efficiency.   KV Cache Spec Decode Batching  
 Scale-Out Systems Distributed Inference & Parallelism  Collective communication and model-parallel execution for multi-GPU and multi-node inference.   Collectives TP / PP / EP Multi-node  
 Architectures Transformers  Transformer implementations in NumPy and PyTorch, including attention variants and efficient architectures.   Attention PyTorch NumPy  
 Generative Models Diffusion  Denoising models, schedulers, U-Nets, latent diffusion, and efficient sampling experiments.   DDPM U-Net Sampling  
 Learning Systems Reinforcement Learning  Policy gradients, actor-critic methods, value learning, and RL systems experiments.   PPO DQN Actor-Critic  
 Foundations DNN from Scratch  Core neural network building blocks from scratch, from ANN and CNN layers to training loops.   NumPy Backprop Training

title: Reinforcement Learning
text:
Reinforcement learning projects focused on value learning, policy optimization, actor-critic training, and scalable experiment loops.


  DQN Replay buffers, target networks, epsilon-greedy control, and value learning 
  Policy Gradients REINFORCE, baselines, reward normalization, and trajectory sampling 
  Actor-Critic Advantage estimation, value heads, entropy regularization, and stable updates 
  PPO Clipped objectives, rollout collection, minibatch updates, and policy evaluation

text:
Operator-level performance work across CUDA C++, Triton, and CuTe DSL.


  LayerNorm Kernel Triton, CuTe DSL 
  Vector Addition Triton, CUDA C++ 
  GEMM CUDA C++, Triton, CuTe DSL

title: Puzzles
text:
Practice tracks for sharpening GPU programming, ML systems intuition, algorithmic problem solving, and quantitative reasoning.


 GPU Programming LeetGPU  Browser-based GPU programming challenges for building comfort with kernels, memory movement, and parallel execution.   GPU Kernels Practice  
 Tensor Systems Tensara  Tensor and systems-oriented exercises for practicing the reasoning patterns behind modern AI workloads.   Tensors Systems AI  
 Mojo / GPU Mojo GPU Puzzles  Hands-on GPU puzzles in Mojo for learning kernel structure, memory hierarchy, and accelerator programming.   Mojo GPU Kernels  
 GPU Architecture GPU Demystified  A practical path for building intuition about GPU architecture, programming models, and performance behavior.   Architecture Performance CUDA  
 CUDA Intuition Sasha Rush GPU Puzzles  Interactive GPU puzzles by Sasha Rush for learning CUDA-style programming through small, focused exercises.   Numba CUDA Colab  
 Algorithms LeetCode  Core algorithm and data-structure practice for interviews, implementation fluency, and problem decomposition.   DSA Interviews Practice  
 Math + Code Project Euler  Mathematical programming problems that reward clean reasoning, efficient algorithms, and numerical insight.   Math Algorithms Reasoning  
 Quant Reasoning Jane Street Puzzles  High-quality probability, logic, and optimization puzzles for practicing precise quantitative thinking.   Logic Probability Optimization  
 Problem Solving Brain Stellar  A puzzle archive for building broad reasoning strength across math, logic, and programming-style challenges.   Puzzles Logic Math

title: Transformers
text:
Transformer projects covering from-scratch implementations, PyTorch models, attention mechanics, architecture variants, and efficiency-focused experiments.


  Transformer in NumPy Tokenization, embeddings, attention, MLP blocks, layer norm, and training loop basics 
  Transformer in PyTorch Clean encoder/decoder implementation with modular attention and training utilities 
  Attention Variants MHA, MQA, GQA, sliding-window attention, rotary embeddings, and KV-cache-aware decoding 
  Efficient Architectures Long-context, sparse, linear-attention, and memory-efficient transformer experiments

title: DNN from Scratch
text:
Implementing core deep learning layers and training mechanics from first principles, with each project ready to link to its GitHub repository as the code is published.


  ANN Fully connected networks, backpropagation, activations, and optimizers in NumPy 
  CNN Convolution, pooling, im2col, batch norm, and image-classification training loops 
  RNN Sequence modeling, truncated BPTT, embeddings, and recurrent hidden state dynamics 
  LSTM Gates, cell state, gradient flow, and sequence prediction experiments

title: Distributed Inference & Parallelism
text:
Scale-out inference projects around communication primitives, process groups, parallelism strategies, placement, and bandwidth-aware serving.


  Collective Communication all-reduce, all-gather, reduce-scatter, all-to-all, broadcast, process groups, and async communication 
  Tensor Parallelism Sharding attention and MLP layers across GPUs for large-model inference 
  Pipeline Parallelism Layer partitioning, microbatching, stage scheduling, and cross-node serving 
  Expert Parallelism MoE expert placement, token routing, all-to-all dispatch, and load balancing 
  Context Parallelism Long-context sequence sharding for attention and KV-cache-heavy workloads 
  Data / Replica Parallelism Serving replicas, request routing, throughput scaling, and failover 
  Topology-Aware Serving Placement, communication overlap, bandwidth bottlenecks, and multi-node profiling

title: Profiling
text:
Profiling workflows for finding bottlenecks across Python, PyTorch operators, CUDA kernels, memory allocation, and full CPU/GPU timelines.


  Nsight Systems System-wide CPU/GPU timelines, CUDA API traces, NVTX ranges, and utilization gaps 
  Nsight Compute Kernel-level metrics, occupancy, memory throughput, warp stalls, and source correlation 
  PyTorch Profiler Operator costs, CPU/CUDA activity, stack traces, tensor shapes, memory, and traces 
  CUDA Memory Profiling Allocator snapshots, fragmentation, peak memory, and non-PyTorch allocation debugging

title: DL Compilers
text:
Compiler-oriented projects around graph capture, IR lowering, fusion, scheduling, generated kernels, and deployment-oriented compilation.


  MLIR IR design, dialects, lowering passes, and code generation pipelines 
  XLA Graph compilation, HLO-style transformations, and accelerator execution 
  TVM Tensor program optimization, scheduling, autotuning, and lowering 
  torch.compile TorchDynamo graph capture, Inductor codegen, graph breaks, and guard behavior

title: Inference Optimization
text:
Techniques for improving serving latency, throughput, memory efficiency, time-to-first-token, and sustained decode performance.


  Quantization FP8, INT8, INT4, AWQ/GPTQ-style compression, and accuracy-performance tradeoffs 
  KV Cache Optimization Paged KV blocks, reuse across requests, offloading, eviction, MQA, and GQA 
  Prefix Caching Automatic prefix reuse for shared prompts and retrieval-heavy workloads 
  Continuous Batching Dynamic scheduling for mixed prefill/decode workloads and higher GPU utilization 
  Speculative Decoding Draft-and-verify decoding, EAGLE-style methods, and acceptance-rate tuning 
  Disaggregated Serving Separating prefill and decode paths for scalable, latency-aware inference

title: Diffusion
text:
Diffusion model projects from denoising fundamentals to latent generation and sampling efficiency.


  DDPM from Scratch Forward noising, denoising objective, beta schedules, and image generation loop 
  U-Net Denoiser Residual blocks, time embeddings, attention blocks, and multi-scale feature maps 
  Latent Diffusion Autoencoder latents, conditioning, classifier-free guidance, and image synthesis 
  Fast Sampling DDIM-style sampling, scheduler comparisons, and quality-latency tradeoffs
